description: CLI-first council mode that launches three isolated agents through Chau7 MCP, applies editable member personas, collects user-approved evidence, resolves majority/veto verdicts, and writes replay/share artifacts
SwiftPM executable supporting magi/MAGI direct questions, ask, doctor, config, replay, and local share generation with Chau7 MCP orchestration
Scripts/install-magi-cli.sh builds the SwiftPM CLI and installs magi into ~/.local/bin by default, with uppercase MAGI supported through case-insensitive resolution or a case-sensitive alias
Run checkpoints, failure categories, interrupted status, denied-evidence counts, complete required artifact bundles, and failure metadata in replay/share outputs make partial MAGI runs inspectable

Missing Chau7/MCP, provider launch failures, agent timeouts, malformed structured output, denied evidence, veto/deadlock outcomes, interrupts, and partial artifact replay paths produce clean statuses and local logs

Each run writes technical.jsonl next to the required artifacts with correlated run/member/tab events for launches, prompt visibility, prompt submission, agent-running checks, polling, parsing, repairs, checkpoints, and failures
Launch checks accept raw running status and responsive provider output when the effective tab status lags, without treating echoed prompt text as proof of a running agent

agent_launch waits for a recognizable provider input surface in the live tab buffer before typing, and launch verification does not treat provider-level PTY logs as proof that a specific tab received the prompt
agent_launch caps prompt-injection waits and reports agent_exited_before_prompt when a provider command exits before receiving the prompt, avoiding opaque MAGI MCP socket timeouts
MAGI polls Chau7 tab-scoped AI completion events alongside PTY output so completed council responses can be parsed from agent turn notifications when scrollback misses the final structured block
magi ask verifies that the running Chau7 MCP socket supports filtered full-message repo events before launching agents and fails immediately with a restart instruction when the app process is stale

Running magi/MAGI without arguments opens an interactive terminal home screen with progressive boot output, ASCII art, colored member panels after boot, boot-status lines ending on the selected council, an ask prompt, help, doctor access, and --config navigation
--config opens an editable terminal panel for member provider/class settings, global evidence/web/deadlock/veto switches, provider checks, and persona file checks
magi config renders provider and model-class choices as numbered lists in interactive terminals and reports stdin/stdout TTY state when the wizard cannot open
magi config can apply one provider/class choice to Melchior, Balthasar, and Casper while keeping the per-member path available for mixed-provider councils
Per-session input latency measures keystroke-to-echo responsiveness only; command submission (Enter) is excluded for every session so a slow command's runtime is never miscounted as UI lag

AI run history and full transcripts in runs.db are pruned at launch to a user-set window (default 30 days, 0 = keep forever, set in Settings > Logs & History), cascading to turns/tool_calls/latency samples and reclaiming disk with a full VACUUM

Append-only hook event logs (claude-events.jsonl, .ai-events.log) are compacted to their most recent slice at monitor start before tailing, so the transient queues can't grow unbounded; durable history stays in the telemetry database

Thin Husky hooks delegate to pnpm quality modes backed by a central registry, affected-surface pre-push scoping, conservative full-suite triggers, content-hash caching, reproducible failure commands, dirty-worktree handling, and runner/registry tests
Recognizes AI tools via process-tree signals and terminal output scanning; uses strictly tuned patterns (e.g. CLI-style indicators) to prevent false positives from generic prose or analytics logs
Generic AI, terminal-session, fallback-history, shell, app, API proxy, and unknown-source events share one tested mapped-source adaptation path with explicit routing and reliability policy
App and shell event producers publish through a narrow AIEventPublishing boundary, keeping detection decoupled from concrete AppModel storage and notification state

Pure tested policy and overlay reconciler derive tab attention from waitingForInput/approvalRequired session state so highlights are repaired independently of banner delivery; attentionReport diagnostics expose state/style mismatches
Terminal-side AI prompt detection emits lower-confidence attention events as soon as a TUI appears blocked, closing provider-hook delays while preserving authoritative hook precedence
Waiting-input and attention-required events keep persistent tab highlights even when duplicate suppression, rate limits, or disabled idle actions suppress follow-up notification delivery
Custom terminal emulator written in Rust with FFI bridge — fast, memory-safe, and correct
Rust exposes alternate-screen state through FFI and debug snapshots so Swift can handle full-screen TUI surfaces without provider-specific heuristics
Pure Chau7Core policy routes scroll input to shell scrollback, mouse-aware TUI apps, or transcript history based on terminal runtime state
Each terminal session keeps a bounded PTY transcript ring with command-boundary backfill so late AI detection can seed accurate session logs
Alternate-screen TUIs without normal scrollback can show recent transcript history on scroll-up while mouse-reporting TUIs keep receiving wheel events
Selected tabs now render through one live surface only; snapshot and cursor handoff overlays no longer stack on top of the live terminal during switches
Non-selected restored tabs stay deferred until selected instead of auto-restoring and mutating visible tabs immediately after launch
Deferred background tabs hydrate provider/session facts for routing and titles without activating live AI render state, restoring command blocks, or queuing resume input until selected
Tab autosave dual-writes a split restore bundle with manifest identity/layout/resume fields and SHA-256-verified sidecar context files for heavier scrollback and command data

Default text side panels attach to the tab-scoped .chau7/sessions/<tab-id>/note.md when opened, auto-save edits, and silently flush dirty content on close. Regular non-auto-save files prompt save/discard/cancel via one shared dialog so the X button and ⌃⌘W agree and Don't Save actually discards.
Shell-reported repo-root changes swap sessions onto the shared model for that root before applying branch updates, preventing branch labels from one repo leaking into another
Branch, repo-root, exit, and foreign notification OSC 9 messages are buffered across PTY chunks before dispatch, so startup metadata survives split terminal reads
Live git probes and shell OSC 9 branch reports treat the detached HEAD sentinel as no branch and clear cached branch identity instead of showing HEAD as a branch
Background identity hydration backs off during rapid tab switching, prioritizes pending tabs nearest to the selected tab, and logs restore-stage timings plus RSS deltas for startup diagnostics
Reveal restored windows after a short splash delay instead of waiting for the full restore queue to drain
Restored scrollback is replayed through the shell again, with restore-artifact filtering preserved, to avoid fresh post-relaunch history corruption while keeping fast visible startup
Metal renders default or explicit emoji presentation including achromatic FE0F symbols with embedded color while keeping terminal UI symbols and box drawing tintable by ANSI foreground color

Terminal-output syntax highlighter caches results in NSCache (real bounded LRU with cost-based eviction and OS-pressure hook) instead of a dictionary with order-unspecified prefix-eviction, so hot lines stay cached on busy streams
Active tab drops to ~10 Hz after idle, snaps back instantly on PTY data or user input (keys, mouse, scroll, paste, IME) — cuts wakeups and CPU on idle AI sessions without any perceptible lag

Only the key window owns live selected-tab presentation; visible selected tabs in main-but-not-key or otherwise non-input-priority windows keep a retained passive surface and use the shared background drain instead of driving full live Metal sync
User setting caps focused-tab refresh at Display Native / 60 Hz / 30 Hz; default follows the screen's native refresh, lower caps trade scroll fluidity for battery
Background tabs release NSImage snapshot caches and mark Metal textures/buffers volatile on demotion, letting the OS reclaim GPU memory under pressure with atlas rebuild on promotion
Suspended tabs keep a retained last-rendered frame so tab switches can show an immediate snapshot while the live terminal resynchronizes
Snapshot-backed tab switches keep the retained frame visible until the selected terminal reports its first live sync, avoiding brief grey flashes during cold-tab reactivation
If a cold tab still has a retained Rust terminal view but no cached frame, Chau7 now captures a retained snapshot on selection so reused terminal views avoid a blank grey handoff
16-color, 256-color, and 24-bit true color with full SGR attributes (bold, italic, underline, strikethrough)
Option-generated programming characters like brackets and braces are routed as literal text on international keyboard layouts
Full Kitty progressive enhancement keyboard protocol for modern key handling
iTerm2 (ESC ] 1337), Sixel, and Kitty image protocol — display images directly in your terminal
Block, underline, bar — with optional blinking

Configurable scrollback size with GPU-accelerated scrolling; active, passive-visible, and warm tabs preserve the configured capacity across render phases, while hidden tabs flush and verify the disk cache before RAM reclamation
Zsh, Bash, Fish, or custom shell path — Apple Silicon and Intel native
Proper dead key, accent, and diacritic input via NSTextInputClient marked text
Configurable default working directory for new terminals
Run custom commands automatically when a new terminal session starts
Tracks working directory changes via OSC 7 escape sequences
Selected text is automatically copied to the clipboard
Option+click to position the cursor anywhere on the command line
Hardware-accelerated text rendering via Apple Metal — smooth even at high throughput
Pure geometry contract and tests keep CPU and Metal rows, columns, cursor cells, mouse mapping, and remainder pixels aligned
Latest-frame-wins sync and present counters expose how many obsolete intermediate frames were skipped during heavy AI-output bursts
Font, grid, zero-size, drawable, zero-cell, and commit failures retry safely with sampled diagnostics and recovery reset after the next committed frame

Bug reports include window content size, terminal surface and grid geometry, rows and columns, cell size, point and pixel remainders, Metal view and drawable size, frame age, coalescing counters, and retry state
Tracked parity matrix for wide glyphs, emoji fallback, ligatures, OSC8 links, selection, local echo overlays, inline images, and command-block tinting with covered partial or external-overlay status

feature: Metal OSC8 and Local Echo Parity
description: Metal sync overlays predicted local-echo cells before GPU conversion, immediately invalidates the active Metal surface on overlay updates and clears, and renders OSC8 link underlines when no explicit SGR underline is present
Session autosave captures the recent ANSI-styled terminal tail through Rust and reuses the versioned snapshot while panes are idle, avoiding repeated multi-megabyte full-buffer work
Forced selected-tab reveal timeouts keep the next real Metal visible-frame signal armed so startup restore records real frames instead of synthesizing 5s fallbacks
Bypass the macOS compositor entirely for GPU-to-display output
Dynamic glyph cache eliminates redundant text rasterization
16–32 byte SIMD-accelerated ANSI escape sequence parsing in Rust for maximum parse throughput
SPSC lock-free PTY data pipeline — zero contention between read and render threads
Triple-buffered terminal state with atomic swaps — no tearing and no render blocking
IOKit HID keyboard input bypasses NSEvent queue for sub-10ms input latency
Mach real-time thread policy for render and input threads
Pre-cache likely terminal output to reduce display latency
Only re-render changed terminal regions — saves GPU cycles on partial updates
Per-feature timing metrics with os.signpost integration for performance analysis
Instantly recognizes Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex, ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, Aider, Cursor
Tab label and logo are derived live from the shell's process tree so a tab restored as Codex flips to Claude within ~1.5s when that's what's actually running

An output-pattern match only originates a new AI identity on a tab when a live process-tree signal corroborates it (otherwise it merely confirms an existing identity), and API-endpoint/website substrings are excluded from fingerprints, so incidental output like an OpenAI URL in a git push can't flag a plain shell as ChatGPT
Each detected AI agent gets its branded logo displayed in the tab
Tabs automatically adopt the brand color of the active AI agent
Define custom patterns to detect and theme any CLI tool with display name and tab color
Smart detection with wrapper skipping (env, sudo, command, builtin, exec, noglob, time)
Detect AI tools from output banners — command-based detection gates output scanning to prevent false positives
One-click error analysis via OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, or custom endpoint — plain-language root cause and fix suggestions
Monitor Claude Code hook events: prompts, tools, permissions, responses — first-class agentic workflow support

Notifications for finished, failed, permission, needs_validation, tool_complete, session_end, idle, and related AI events; default AI policy enables only finished, failed, and permission requests; visible banners include repo tab or directory context when available
Codex and Claude idle detection now come from one common monitor-source list instead of duplicated setup blocks reducing drift between provider-specific history monitoring paths
Provider-specific events are translated into one shared semantic notification model before settings, history, styling, and delivery run
Claimed explicit Codex session matches are reused across history growth so autosave avoids repeated fallback rescans and unresolved resume-metadata log spam

Restored tabs persist explicit observed and clearly marked synthetic AI session IDs plus launch command agent start time last input time and shell exit metadata for accurate resume behavior and audits

Notification delivery runtime strict-session lookup and history adoption use a cached cross-window routing index built from live sessions and deferred restore metadata before falling back to recovery heuristics
MCP tab_create defaults to the active Chau7 overlay window so externally created tabs appear in the currently visible window without an explicit window_id
Runtime launches that require a user-visible Chau7 tab now fail explicitly with structured metadata instead of silently succeeding in a hidden PTY-only path
The embedded server negotiates supported MCP protocol versions and requires initialize plus notifications/initialized before tools or resources can be used
Malformed tool requests now return JSON-RPC protocol errors, while execution failures return result.isError with structured content for machine-readable recovery
tools/call applies per-tool token-bucket rate limiting with higher budgets for polling and status endpoints to reduce local abuse without breaking normal orchestration loops
Agent state distinguishes done from idle, exposes approvalRequired as a first-class status, and projects pre-derived logo and tab-color metadata to remote consumers
Shell, app, terminal-session, history-monitor, events-log, and API-proxy events now pass through the same canonical adapter boundary instead of bypassing the shared notification model
Provider hook names normalize through one shared mapper that preserves semantic separators so values like permission prompt idle-prompt and auth success resolve to the same canonical trigger keys
Claude notification hooks own waiting-input and attention-required delivery; raw response_complete stays state-only

Runtime and hook-backed finished failed and permission events take priority, while history-derived events act only as fallback and are suppressed after an authoritative delivery for the same session or tab; the four time-windowed suppression maps and the routing-retry counter live on a dedicated NotificationDeliveryPolicy that returns pass/drop/scheduleRetry verdicts so the manager's processEvent stays a thin orchestrator

Authoritative AI events retry exact tab routing before broader fallback matching to reduce missed highlights and startup restore races; explicit-tab rebinds round-trip every field via AIEvent.replacingTabID so repoPath survives even when the session-resolver corrects an explicit tab ID
Authoritative Claude events can recover exact routing from live split-session metadata when runtime ownership is missing, instead of dropping permission and waiting-input delivery immediately

Notification style delivery verifies that the explicit target tab still exists and suppresses vanished-tab noise once exact-session recovery is exhausted; the live-tab lookup, deferred retry, auto-clear timer, and redundant-re-apply suppression live on a dedicated StyleTabCoordinator instead of being interleaved with 23 other action implementations

Shell-out actions enforce their configured timeout with SIGTERM then SIGKILL via a shared ProcessRunner so trap-immune or I/O-blocked scripts can't hang; webhook / Slack / Discord outbound HTTP routes through a dedicated ephemeral URLSession with 15s request and 30s resource timeouts and no shared cookie storage

Every notification action is implemented as a NotificationActionHandler registered in a per-type registry; 25 actions are grouped into 7 category files (Basic / Automation / Integration / DevOps / Productivity / Accessibility / TimeTracking) and the executor is a thin dispatcher. Adding a new action is one handler type plus one registry entry with no executor edits.

NotificationManager consults a single NotificationDeliveryHost protocol for tab title repo name active-tab check and tab routing; TerminalControlService conforms and the app wires it via setHost at startup; replaces five separate closure properties.

NotificationManager and NotificationActionExecutor are constructed via a single NotificationServices composition root with no .shared singletons; AppModel holds the bundle as an injected services reference and view-layer callsites use NotificationServices.current.

Exact Claude session routing tolerates repo-root vs nested-subdirectory cwd differences and providerless live tab metadata, keeping authoritative permission and waiting-input events bound to the right tab
Delayed notification style clearing re-checks tab liveness and can recover once via exact session lookup instead of logging stale tab failures after a valid notification
AI-first notification overview for Finished Failed and Permission Request with direct banner tab highlight sound and dock bounce controls; advanced trigger and per-tool rules remain available
Waiting-input and attention-required states are managed as needs-me attention instead of being grouped under generic finished events
Each AI notification records delivery state, coalescing, retries, drop reasons, resolved tab identity, and banner or style outcomes for deterministic debugging

Provider events are canonicalized before the delivery ledger and coalescing queue record them, so shared history and timeline surfaces use canonical semantics with raw-type notes retained for debugging
Unified event publication and notification delivery now share one accepted-event handoff so events are canonicalized once before they are displayed and delivered

Notification ingress flows through one shared contract and tab-targeting actions only execute with an explicit resolved tabID, eliminating late overlay re-resolution and NotificationCenter side-channel delivery

Shipped defaults notify only when an agent is waiting on you (permission/waiting_input/attention_required/elicitation) or has finished working (finished/failed/response_failed); shell command completion, AI idle, and tool_failed are off by default
Default agent finished/approval/feedback notifications bounce the Dock icon - informational (single bounce) for completion, critical (bounces until focused) for approval and feedback
Permission waiting-input idle and attention-required events for the same unresolved session are suppressed for a cooldown window instead of re-alerting continuously
Once a session emits finished or failed, later permission waiting-input idle and attention-required events for that same identity are dropped as stale
System resume prefills do not generate prompt-return waiting-input notifications during launch or restore; fallback attention resumes only after the next real user command
Restored AI tabs suppress prompt-return waiting-input fallback as soon as provider metadata is restored, preventing startup highlights before resume-prefill delivery
Notification delivery records whether focus style badge and snippet actions actually succeeded instead of assuming success once requested, making logs and ledger entries fully explainable
MCP-created runtime sessions transition to ready after launch, and attach_tab_id sessions start usable immediately
Repeated runtime approval timeouts recover only for a bounded number of attempts before the session records a session_error and moves to failed
Runtime journals record user_input boundaries, correlated tool use and results, turn-scoped output chunks, duration metadata, and per-turn event queries for deterministic orchestration
Model-less Claude Codex and Gemini runs still receive estimated cost using provider-default pricing families instead of dropping to unavailable
Repository turn summaries preserve the last completed turn's tokens tools duration exit reason and estimated cost after the session goes idle
Runtime sessions emit cost_threshold journal events when cumulative estimated cost crosses configured limits
Agent dashboard cards track cache creation and cache read tokens separately instead of collapsing all cached usage into reads
Permission checks parse background separators, tabs, and newlines before applying allow/block/approval rules
Runtime backend launch strings drop invalid environment variable names before shell interpolation
History-monitor idle and finished events resolve provider session metadata to recover the working directory before notification routing
Notification routing prefers exact AI session ID matches before broader provider and title heuristics
Newly detected cross-tab file conflicts emit app events for each affected tab
Real-time tailing of ~/.ai-events.log for AI tool events
Capture raw terminal output for AI tool sessions (codex-pty and claude-pty logs)
Maps Codex OpenAI capture sessions to working directories with a bounded metadata cache
Monitor and optimize LLM API token usage in real time — per tab or globally
File-backed reads and stdin-backed reads share one tested filtering, truncation, non-empty-output preservation, and line-number formatting path to prevent parser drift
chau7-optim read preserves original output when filtering would collapse a non-empty file, stdin payload, or selected range to empty output, preventing optimized read commands from looking broken
Real-time CTO metrics: decision counts, mode changes, deferred operations, health assessment
Manages per-tab CTO flag files that AI tools read to adjust context behavior
Codex and other OpenAI-family telemetry runs finalize immediately at run end, then backfill richer transcript and tool-call content asynchronously when provider extraction completes
History maintenance operations serialize on the DB queue so queued async inserts cannot repopulate after `clearAll()`; regression tests cover the race
Embedded JSON-RPC 2.0 server on Unix socket (~/.chau7/mcp.sock) — AI agents can control your terminal
Full MCP protocol compliance with tools and resources capabilities
Stdio-to-socket bridge binary installed at ~/.chau7/bin/chau7-mcp-bridge for standard MCP client compatibility
Automatically registers Chau7 MCP server in ~/.claude.json when Claude Code is installed
Automatically registers in ~/.cursor/mcp.json when Cursor is installed
Automatically registers in ~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json when Windsurf is installed
Automatically registers in ~/.codex/config.toml when Codex config exists
Global on/off toggle for the MCP server (mcpEnabled setting)
Optional approval dialog before MCP operations touch your terminal (mcpRequiresApproval)
Configurable maximum MCP-created tabs per session (default 4 — hard cap 50)
Purple indicator badge on MCP-controlled tabs (mcpShowTabIndicator)
List all open tabs across all windows with status, cwd, git branch, CTO state, active app; primary live discovery API for active AI tabs
Open a new terminal tab with optional directory and target window — respects approval gate and tab limit and returns exec-acceptance plus prompt-readiness fields
Execute a shell command in a tab — auto-queues when shell/bootstrap state still needs to settle so launchers can submit deterministically without waiting for prompt-ready rendering

Detailed live tab status: process state, child processes (PID/CPU/RSS), active telemetry run, git branch, ai_provider, ai_session_id, deterministic exec-acceptance fields via can_accept_exec/exec_acceptance_mode, and stricter prompt-ready fields via ready_for_exec/readiness_reason
Wait until tab_exec will be accepted (can_accept_exec=true) and return the last observed status snapshot on success or timeout
Send raw input to terminal for interactive prompts and confirmations — no auto-newline
Send terminal key presses for interactive TUIs — Enter, Escape, arrows, backspace, delete, paging keys, and ctrl/alt combos
Submit the current interactive prompt by sending Enter as a key press
Close a tab with optional force flag — checks for running processes before closing
Get terminal output (max 10000 lines, 512KB cap). source=pty_log returns full ANSI-stripped AI session. wait_for_stable_ms polls buffer.
Set per-tab CTO override (default/forceOn/forceOff) — recalculates flag files and records decision
Set a custom title for a tab — pass empty string to clear
Get metadata for a repository including description, labels, favorite files, and frequent commands
Set metadata for a repository (description, labels, favorite files) — only provided fields are updated
Get frequently used commands for a repository, sorted by frecency
Get recent AI tool events (finished, permission, tool_called, etc.) scoped to a given repo
Get a single telemetry run by ID (active or from store), including run_state and content_state for live vs finalized data
List telemetry runs with filters: session_id, repo_path, provider, parent_run_id, date range, tags, limit/offset; active runs are deduplicated before pagination
Get all tool calls for a telemetry run — see exactly what an AI agent did
Get conversation transcript for a run — active Codex runs use ~/.codex/history.jsonl first, then ANSI-stripped PTY log for TUI tools, then terminal buffer
Set tags on a telemetry run for organization and filtering
Get the most recent run for a repository — optionally filter by provider
List telemetry/history AI sessions with run counts — filter by repo_path, active_only; includes active/completed rollups and latest_run_state. Use tab_list/tab_status for live discovery
Get currently active telemetry-backed AI sessions. Use tab_list/tab_status for live discovery and control
Get Chau7 build and process identity for external observability: version, build metadata, process id, launch time, and schema version
Get recent Chau7 observability events with stable sequence ids. Includes app lifecycle markers and unified non-app AI events with optional tab/session/run/repo scoping
Get Chau7-owned timer and display-link inventory with stable ids, subsystem, queue label, cadence, and active state

Get the authoritative aggregated observer snapshot: runtime identity, live tabs, pending approvals, repo event summaries, active telemetry runs/sessions, timers, latest sequence, and observer contract metadata

Open one long-lived Chau7 state subscription on the current MCP connection. Returns an initial snapshot plus optional replayed changes since a cursor, exposes subscription health metadata, and emits JSON-RPC notifications/chau7.event deltas plus heartbeat keepalives
Stop the active Chau7 state subscription for the current MCP connection
MCP no longer accepts runtime_* tool calls; live control is tab_* and history/analytics are run_* and session_*
Quota snapshots and rate-limit windows are extracted from both one-line JSONL and pretty-printed multiline Codex rollout JSON objects
Runtime-managed AI runs persist parent run/session lineage and task metadata into telemetry so parent agents can fetch exact child runs instead of inferring by repo-wide recency
Code review task templates can review explicit staged patches and staged file lists, not only commit ranges, while preserving structured findings and review-safe policy defaults
Runtime-managed sessions remain app-internal for dashboard and review flows and are no longer part of the public MCP tool surface
MCP session create/stop and tab create/close flows record focused main-thread stall telemetry with operation context for beachball investigations
Read-only resource: latest 20 telemetry run summaries
Read-only resource: AI session index with metadata
Read-only resource: currently active AI sessions
Dynamic resource: specific run details by ID
Capture every LLM API call — tokens in/out, cost, latency, model, endpoint
Go-based proxy (chau7-proxy) intercepts API calls to Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic with TLS and WebSocket support
Count input/output tokens per call and calculate aggregate cost
Record API response times for performance analysis
Auto-detect AI task candidates from API activity with confidence scoring — approve or fail with notes
Calculate token savings from context caching strategies
Command stats and error rates and API usage and timing in a unified dashboard view
Recent proxy calls in Debug Console analytics show local hour, repo name, and endpoint context for each call
Per-repository stats (commands, success rate, AI runs, tokens, cost, providers, top tools) in Debug Console Repos tab, Data Explorer, and hover card
Visual timeline scrubber showing command blocks and metrics over time
Include or exclude specific API providers from capture
Unlimited tabs per window — Cmd+T to create, Cmd+1–9 to jump
Custom tab names via Cmd+Option+R
12+ colors: blue, teal, green, red, orange, pink, purple, yellow, gray, and more
Drag tabs or Cmd+Opt+Shift+[/] to move left/right — center-crossing snap thresholds keep sibling motion aligned with the grabbed tab
Current git branch displayed in the tab — no need to type git branch. In protected folders, the last known branch remains visible from cached repo identity until live access is granted again.
Working directory visible in the tab title
Tab shows last command name with duration and exit status
Type once and send to all tabs — with per-tab exclusion and visual indicator
Cmd+Opt+W to close all tabs except the current one
Configure where new tabs appear (after current or at end)
Inactive tabs suspend rendering after configurable delay to save resources
Cmd+D — split side-by-side with draggable dividers

The side-panel text editor hides the horizontal scroll bar when word wrap is on (default) and re-tiles the line-number gutter when its width changes, so wrapped text never shows a spurious lateral scroll; no-wrap mode keeps the horizontal scroller for long lines
Cmd+Opt+D — stack panes vertically
Arbitrary nesting depth via binary tree layout controller
Persisted split-pane trees carry a schema version so future binaries can add pane kinds without older code silently mis-decoding snapshots; pre-versioned snapshots read as version 1 with no migration

MainScheduler / Polling / Dialogs protocols injected into SplitPaneController and TextEditorModel; the close-time dialog branches and the markdown runbook sequential runner are unit-driveable headlessly via FakeDialogs and a virtual-time TestMainScheduler — no AppKit modal loops or real sleeps required

Six pane kinds (Terminal, TextEditor, FilePreview, DiffViewer, Repository, Dashboard) conform to one PaneNode protocol owning kind/hasUnsavedWork/dispose; the SplitNode enum migration onto this protocol collapses ~17 hand-rolled 7-case switches into 2-case ones

SplitNode is `.leaf(any PaneNode)` + `.split(...)`; three visitor primitives (collectLeaves / findLeaf / walkLeaves) back every per-kind accessor (allTerminalIDs, findFirstEditor, firstPaneID(ofType:), etc.) so adding a new pane kind is a PaneNode conformance plus one view case, not a sweep across dozens of switches
Each PaneNode conformer owns its savedRepresentation() so adding a new pane kind is a protocol method override; the central encode switch on SplitNode collapsed to a one-liner delegating to the leaf

Two responsibility clusters peeled off SplitPaneController: PaneCloseConfirmer owns the close-time save/discard/cancel dialog policy, SessionNoteCoordinator owns the .chau7/sessions/<tabID>/note.md path math and idempotent prepare step. Both have focused unit-test surfaces and the controller's session-note + close methods collapse to thin delegations.

RunbookCodeBlockTracker (@Observable) owns the markdown-runbook state machine and sequential runner; EditorAutoSaver owns the debounced save and status-clear work items. Both peel off from TextEditorModel into focused, unit-testable types and the model exposes them as editor.runbook + editor.autoSaver respectively.

Value type owning per-directory commit-message draft persistence (load/save/clear) + conventional-commit prefix list + applyPrefix/hasConventionalPrefix rules. UserDefaults is injected so the store is unit-driveable against a scratch suite. Extracted from RepositoryPaneModel.

History section (commits, stashes, historySearchText, filteredCommits) is a dedicated @Observable accessed as repo.history; search-text bumps no longer fan invalidation out to status/commit/branches sections through the outer model.

Shared header skeleton (icon + title + close + 2 ViewBuilder slots for embellishments) replaces hand-rolled HStacks in FilePreviewPaneView and DiffViewerPaneView; same shape can absorb TextEditor/Repository headers later.

Five pane callbacks (onFocus, onUpdateRatio, onClosePane, onFilePathClicked, onRunCommand) bundle into one PaneEnvironment value exposed via @Environment instead of being threaded through every SplitNodeView initializer.

Test suite iterates every PaneNode conformer and asserts the contract invariants (id/kind agreement, dispose idempotency, persistence round-trip, hasUnsavedWork policy, existential cast round-trip) so a new pane kind extends one fixture and inherits every invariant automatically.

Reusable test bundle exposing every PaneNode contract invariant as a focused assertion function plus a single assertContract entry; the parametrized driver runs the full kit over every pane kind × edit state × persistence round-trip.

All four pane headers (TextEditor / FilePreview / DiffViewer / Repository) share one PaneHeaderBar component; restructured with a title ViewBuilder slot so interactive titles (tap-to-copy filename, branch-picker button) can opt in.
MarkdownRunbookView takes one RunbookHost protocol instead of five separate closures; RunbookHostAdapter bridges TextEditorModel + a send-to-terminal closure into the unified surface.

Decode-side persistence routes through PaneFactoryRegistry keyed on PaneType (each pane class provides static makeFromSaved); adding a new pane kind requires zero edits to existing fromSavedNode code — one registry entry + one static.

All five Repository pane sections (Commit / Status / History / Branches / Session) ride on dedicated @Observable sub-states (repo.commit, repo.status, repo.history, repo.branchState, repo.session) so any per-section mutation re-renders only that view subtree.
Full editor in a split pane (⌘⌥E) — syntax highlighting, line numbers, bracket matching, auto-indent, scroll-to-line, find/replace
Untitled side text panes can save directly to .chau7/sessions/<tab-id>/note.md inside the active repository and reopen the matching note for the tab's current repo on restore

Full git UI in a split pane (⌘⌥B) — session-aware: shows agent-touched files with diff stats when AI active, resets after push. Stage/unstage, commit (⌘Enter), branch, push/pull, stash, history with search, conflict resolution, ahead/behind, hover tooltips, commit chips. Prompts for protected-folder access only when a live git action is requested. Supporting types extracted into a dedicated file as scaffolding toward splitting status/commit/history into independent observable sub-states.
Click the file name in the editor pane header to copy it to the clipboard
Syntax highlighting for HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python, and more
Edit code and run it in the same window — no app switching
⇧⌥⌘E appends the current terminal selection to the side editor; opens the editor on demand if none is open so the shortcut never silently no-ops
Floating terminal layer on top of all other apps with blur background — always accessible
Ctrl+` to summon a dropdown terminal from anywhere — configurable height
Cmd+N for multiple independent terminal windows
Transparent terminal windows — see through to your code
Native macOS fullscreen with configurable toolbar behavior
Strip all chrome — maximum terminal space with zero distraction
Windows reopen exactly where you left them per workspace
All windows and tabs restored on relaunch

Production tab-state backups stay in the stable backup directory while dev and unidentified bundles write to bundle-scoped backup directories; launch retains multi-window recovery state until the next save so additional windows survive early exits
Run as a menu bar app — no Dock icon needed
When Chau7 Remote backgrounds, the session briefly stays alive in approvals-only mode instead of streaming full terminal output
Relay-backed APNs push registration and wakeups for new approvals and interactive prompts on the Chau7 Remote iPhone app
The Chau7 Remote app and widget extension target iOS 18.0 (IPHONEOS_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET = 18.0); the shared SwiftPM package declares an .iOS(.v17) floor for library targets
Optional Rust-backed terminal grid renderer for Chau7 Remote on iPhone, with text fallback retained
macOS streams terminal output and snapshots only for the tab currently selected on iPhone; background tabs stay metadata/activity/approvals-only until switched to
The iPhone app emits os.signpost intervals for frame processing, output append, and ANSI stripping so receive/render lag can be measured in Instruments
Cmd+F — search terminal output with match highlighting
Toggle regex mode for advanced pattern matching
Persistent case-sensitive search preference
Cmd+G / Cmd+Shift+G to cycle through matches
Cmd+E to populate search with current selection
All matches highlighted across terminal output
Intercepts rm -rf, dd, mkfs, and other destructive commands — confirms before execution
Add your own regex patterns to the danger guard
Dangerous commands visually highlighted in output
Cmd+click file paths and URLs — configurable action (browser, editor, Finder) with line:column support
Remap any shortcut with interactive editor and conflict detection
One-click keybinding presets
Cmd+Shift+V — configurable clipboard history, default 50 entries (up to 1000), with LRU eviction, pinning, and quick paste
Auto-escape shell-sensitive characters ($, backticks, quotes, backslash) on paste
Cmd+; to open — create, edit, delete, import, export snippets
Global (user), per-SSH-profile, and per-repo snippets
Numbered tab stops ${1:default} (${0} = final cursor) plus ${cwd}/${home}/${date}/${time}/${clip} tokens
.chau7/config.toml in your repo — share snippets with your team
Arrow keys for local history, Cmd+Up/Down for cross-tab
History survives restarts — searchable and fast
Command frequency, timing, and success rates
Pin important positions in your terminal and navigate back instantly
Native macOS notifications for task completion, failures, permission requests
Animated dock badge and critical/non-critical bounce for key events
Alert sounds (Glass, Purr, etc.) with volume control
Notify when a command has been idle beyond configurable threshold. Fires once per session, resets only on real user activity.
Tabs change color on events — auto-clear after configurable timeout, clear persistent approval styling immediately on approval resolution, and style all tabs affected by a file conflict

The green "finished" tab highlight persists until you open the tab instead of auto-clearing after ~30s; opening the tab clears its non-persistent highlight, matching permission highlights that stay until resolved
Prevent notification spam with configurable thresholds scoped per trigger and tab/session/directory identity
Enable/disable and customize actions for each event type individually
Isolated test mode disables notification-center integration to avoid side effects and startup crashes
Save hosts, ports, identity files, jump hosts (ProxyJump) — all in one place
Watches SSH config and imports new hosts automatically
Settings change automatically based on directory, SSH host, or environment variables
Each profile gets its own color scheme, shell, font, and keybindings
Share a read-only view of your terminal — others see what you see
Approve or deny viewer connections — you control access
Cloudflare Workers relay — no port forwarding required
macOS reduces AI event streams into one remote activity state for clients
Chau7 Remote renders the current AI task in a native Live Activity and Dynamic Island
Detected Claude and Codex terminal prompts appear in the iPhone Approvals tab with option buttons that reply to the correct tab. Destructive options require a second confirmation before sending.
Record terminal sessions with timestamps for replay
Scrub through recorded sessions like a video
16 ANSI colors + background, foreground, cursor, selection — fully customizable
Respects macOS appearance or override manually
System fonts plus popular coding fonts — or use any installed font
8–72pt with per-tab zoom (Cmd++/-, 50–200%)
Fine-tune line height for your preference
Colored left-border gutter marks — green (success), red (fail), blue (running)
Command blocks carry runtime turn IDs and exact terminal row bounds so overlays and investigators can map shell work back to the active agent turn
Optional timestamps on each terminal line in multiple formats
Optional auto-formatting of JSON output in terminal
Tabbed interface with fuzzy search across 100+ settings
Save, load, export, and import named configurations
.chau7/config.toml in any repo — project-specific settings
Auto-reload on config changes — no restart needed
Optional iCloud sync of settings across devices
Fine-grained toggle control over every behavior
Build a separate Chau7 Test.app with isolated storage and keychain namespaces
Auto-detect and import existing Terminal.app profiles
Import iTerm2 profiles and color schemes
Walk through initial configuration on first launch
Contextual tips surface as you use the app
Full macOS accessibility integration
Respects system accessibility preferences
English, French, Spanish, Arabic, Hebrew — with proper RTL layout across all windows
Layout direction set at every NSHostingView boundary — all 13 hosting sites flip for Arabic/Hebrew
Zero untranslated strings across en/fr/ar/he — all UI elements localized: SwiftUI views, NSMenuItem context menus, hover cards, agent dashboard, terminal right-click, and all auxiliary windows

English, French, Arabic, and Hebrew bundles now include the remaining shipped UI keys added by the latest L() migration, including settings search/help copy, dashboard text, alerts, snippets examples, and long-form help topics

After the final locale polish pass, the remaining identical strings across fr ar he are limited to intentional identities such as product names, browser names, file paths, protocol literals, and placeholder-only formats
Change language without restarting the app
Unix socket API for external automation — control tabs, run commands, query history, manage snippets
Scripting socket exposes low-level review building blocks such as create_tab run_command get_tab send_input submit_prompt get_output close_tab and get_repo_events for external automation

Repo-local scripts/pre-commit-review creates a review tab, launches Codex, waits for interactive readiness, sends and submits the staged-diff prompt, polls PTY output for the final JSON block, and prints structured findings with configurable gate and timeout policy
The pre-commit review client uses explicit Unix-socket timeouts so a stalled scripting server fails fast instead of hanging git commit
The pre-commit review flow tears down its review tab after completion or failure without relying on a runtime-backed delegated session wrapper
The local pre-push hook rejects plain release tags like 0.1.1 when the GitHub release workflow is configured for v-prefixed tags only
Save-time Codex resume fallback results are cached per session signature so unchanged unresolved tabs stop repeating the same history scan and unresolved log spam on every autosave
Restore and Metal render paths now record explicit main-thread stall events, and relaunch restore holds a scoped low-latency App Nap lease while startup work settles

Startup restore now coalesces protected-root validation, debounces transient snippet-context refreshes, waits briefly for pane-view readiness before resume-prefill fallback, and emits a one-line restore summary
Query and modify settings programmatically from any language
Repo badges highlight while their dashboard overlay is open, and clicking any tab closes the dashboard again, including the already-selected tab
Reopening the singleton Data Explorer window rebuilds its SwiftUI content so history and telemetry reload
Sessions Explorer rows derive provider and repo labels from the latest run for each session
Cmd+Option+L — live log viewer with 10 tabs: State, Token Optimizer, Events, Lag, Perf, Logs, Report, Analytics, Health, Repos
View live app state: tabs and sessions and models
Per-feature timing metrics with os.signpost integration
Category-based logging with correlation IDs (App, Tabs, Terminal, Render, etc.)

In-app issue reporter (⌥⌘I) with all sensitive data off by default, per-toggle tab pickers, live markdown preview, HTTPS-only private relay submission, success banner with created issue number when available, tab title redaction, background history capture, and hardened rate limiting
GDPR-compliant sub-processor disclosure (Cloudflare, GitHub) with data categories, retention, legal basis (Art. 6(1)(f)), DPA links, and data subject rights
CHAU7_VERBOSE=1 and CHAU7_TRACE=1 for deep diagnostics
Cmd+Option+P for fuzzy-searchable command palette (VS Code style)
Auto-detect running dev servers by command hints, output patterns, and port scanning with 30s liveness polling
Notifications on git branch changes
Custom regex patterns to watch for in terminal output
Notify on working directory changes
Define custom output patterns to trigger actions
Quick access icon with active/paused indicator
Recent events, active sessions, and quick toggles in a popover
Hide from Dock and Cmd+Tab — activation policy .accessory
Keep terminal above other apps — .floating window level
Configurable blink interval (0.3–2.0s) and custom cursor color (hex)
Terminal search toggle wraps query in word boundary anchors
Screen flash on bell with configurable minimum interval between triggers
Match (), [], {}, <> pairs with jump-to-matching-bracket; scan walks NSString UTF-16 in place so cursor moves don't materialise a Character array per keystroke
Absolute, relative, or hybrid line numbers with dynamic gutter width
Auto-switch settings profile on directory change based on configured rules
Standalone export/import for keybindings separate from full settings
~/.chau7/pricing.json overrides built-in model pricing table
FinalTerm shell integration: prompt/command/output region markers with exit codes, parsed in Rust. Suppresses heuristic fallbacks when present.
Drop files to paste paths; Option+drop images for base64 data URIs for AI CLIs

Open .md in editor pane with executable code blocks; Run All sends blocks sequentially, waiting for each to finish before sending the next so a long command never paste-bombs the shell; parsed sections are cached so code-block state ticks do not re-walk the file
PM/dashboard sessions prefer .chau7/sessions/<session-id>/plan.md, keep autosave scoped to Chau7-managed plan files, and track duplicate code-block runs by block position
Split-pane text editors preserve standard macOS cut, copy, and paste shortcuts before terminal-specific fallbacks run
Git diff snapshot per command (OSC 133) shows files modified by AI sessions (Cmd+Option+G)
Without OSC 133, Chau7 still creates command blocks, consumes shell-reported exit status, records user/agent/system input rows, and restores changed-file status for prior commands
Restored tabs retain per-command changed-file metadata and loading status so repository panes do not forget touched files after app restart
Tabs idle beyond configurable threshold (default 10 min) grouped into a dropdown chip in the tab bar with hover card support

Group tabs by git repo (Off/Auto/Manual). Inline repo-name tag chip with connecting line overlay. Suppresses redundant repo path in grouped tab titles, and inherited group membership auto-detaches when a tab moves to a different repo, including tabs opened directly at another directory.
Read-only file viewer with syntax highlighting and image support (Cmd+Opt+O). Lighter than full editor.

Unified git diff view with colored additions/deletions, dual line numbers, Working/Staged toggle (Cmd+Opt+Shift+D). Binary and rename-only changes show a dedicated empty-state ('Binary file' / 'File renamed: old → new') instead of a misleading 'no changes' panel. Requests protected-folder access on demand before loading live git diffs.
Tabs preserve known repo root and last-known branch when macOS protected-folder access blocks live git probing, so passive repo UI and grouping remain stable until live access is granted.

User-initiated live-git actions remember a recent cancel or failed grant per protected root, so Chau7 does not reopen the same Downloads/Desktop/Documents permission panel in a loop when nothing changed.
Open SSH sessions, run commands, change directory, or open files from external apps
All Chau7-specific shortcuts use Cmd+Option; tab selection internationalized (Cmd+1-9)
Anthropic/OpenAI streaming responses correctly parsed for model, tokens, and cost
Cmd+Q lists running process names and confirms before quitting
Multi-character ligature shaping via CoreText (Fira Code, JetBrains Mono, etc.). Configurable toggle.
Time-to-first-token measured per API call via firstByteReader wrapper, stored in SQLite
Flags Cyrillic/fullwidth lookalike characters in commands
Flags pasted commands spanning multiple lines
Scope allowed dangerous commands to specific directories
Adds logging/warning/blocking responses for self-harming commands that target Chau7 or its managed runtime
Allow execution but log unlisted commands for review
Restrict MCP profiles to specific AI agents via agentAllowlist
History persists across restarts via UserDefaults JSON
Case-insensitive substring search across clipboard history
Detect file://, ssh://, ftp://, sftp:// in addition to http/https
All 12 methods implemented: tabs, commands, output, snippets, history, settings, status
Per-provider cost, per-tab tokens, aggregate totals in debug console with repo-aware per-tab labels
Minimap shows command activity hotspots via bucketed opacity overlay
Per-profile toggle: treat Unicode ambiguous-width chars as 1 or 2 cells (Swift + Rust FFI)
TLS and WebSocket proxy for Codex OpenAI API capture
Transparent HTTP/HTTPS proxy for API call capture and analysis
Set custom proxy host and port for API interception
Remote approvals and notifications include repo/branch directory recent command and MCP source notes
Cody now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
ChatGPT now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
Gemini now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
Generic shell backend now shell-escapes each JSON-RPC argument to preserve literal argv boundaries
Runtime Claude events now bind to exact session IDs and preserve multiple same-directory agent tabs without cwd collisions
Claude runtime adoption now uses exact tab session metadata first and refuses ambiguous same-repo bindings instead of guessing by cwd
Pre-release DMG builds now default to an Apple Silicon artifact with a styled drag-to-install layout, optional universal helpers, and a single bundled proxy copy
Restore-time shell maintenance commands stay tagged as system input so waiting-input suppression survives startup, and notification action delegates now return real cross-window success
Codex resume metadata now clears claimed stale explicit session IDs when no deterministic replacement exists, preventing repeated same-repo save and restore conflicts
Tab-style delivery now revalidates stale explicit tab IDs against an exact live session match before failing, so recovered session-bound tabs still highlight after registration drift
Authoritative Claude notification events now keep exact runtime/session binding only and fail closed when the owning tab is ambiguous, instead of inheriting heuristic tab resolution
issues.chau7.sh now serves a lightweight landing page for browsers while still accepting app-submitted bug reports at the root POST endpoint
The compare page now links directly to the hosted Chau7 DMG download, and the shared footer copy reflects the current automated test count
Arabic, French, and Hebrew localizations now include the latest acknowledgments/about stack summary and license summary copy
Remote relay control frames now require encryption after handshake and push POSTs normalize websocket relay URLs to HTTP(S)
Termination clears persisted tab/window state and backup files when every overlay window has been hidden or closed
Notification pipeline now checks action enabled state before using the native showNotification fast path
Closed tabs reopen with their original tab ID, creation timestamp, and repo grouping membership intact
Transcript-derived AI runs now persist separate input, cached-input, output, and reasoning-output token fields with validity/source metadata
Claude/Codex transcript extraction now slices per run, deduplicates repeated usage blocks, and invalidates implausible historical totals
Debug console now separates proxy API-call analytics from transcript-derived AI run telemetry, with explicit cost coverage labeling for missing run pricing
Proxy settings expose an explicit toggle for routing OpenAI-compatible clients such as Codex through the local analytics proxy
Debug console can rebuild transcript-derived Claude/Codex run metrics in place to repair historical token corruption after parser fixes
Codex notify hook installation preserves existing user notify commands, retains authoritative opaque thread IDs, and only disables prompt fallback once Chau7 confirms the hook is installed
Codex notify-hook events now export the owning overlay tab UUID, and authoritative stale explicit tab IDs are corrected via exact session binding before notification delivery
Amazon Q now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
Devin now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
Goose now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
Mentat now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
Amp now has a dedicated normalized AI event source with first-class trigger catalog, settings grouping, and timeline/source labels
In-app help page documenting monorepo layout, languages, Rust crates, bundled binaries, third-party dependencies, system frameworks, and notice files
Moved the remote protocol spec next to services/chau7-remote and split iOS-specific remote UX notes into apps/chau7-ios/docs, removing the orphaned top-level remote-control docs folder
Root docs now define canonical public entry points, collapse stale duplicate onboarding guidance, and remove planning/assessment artifacts from the public repository
Staged doc validation now rejects absolute filesystem links, stale repository URLs, stale moved-doc link targets, and TODO markers in the public root README

Proxy extracts cache creation, cache read, and reasoning tokens from Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini responses (streaming + non-streaming). Dashboard shows full breakdown with hover tooltip and per-agent cost.

Cost calculation accounts for provider-specific cache pricing — Anthropic cache reads 0.1x, writes 1.25x; OpenAI per-model cache-read rates (0.1x–0.5x). Token estimation fallback when extraction fails.
English, Arabic, French, and Hebrew localization bundles now include the missing shipped UI string keys that were still being requested from Swift source
Ambiguous same-repo Codex save-time resolution now preserves a tab's explicit session metadata so restored tabs keep their last-known resume command
Termination now reuses a recent cached multi-window snapshot instead of re-exporting every visible tab during quit
Dashboard polls adaptively — 2s active, 5s idle, 10s no agents — with proxy health monitoring and timeline pagination
~20 new models (GPT-5.x, GPT-4.1, o3/o4-mini, Gemini 2.5), corrected Opus 4.6/Haiku 4.5 prices, fallback warning logging, documented pricing.json schema
Compound cost index, automatic 180-day data pruning, ANALYZE after migrations

Inject content into API requests per repository via ~/.chau7/prompt-rules.json or repo-local .chau7/injection.json — match by repo name (portable) or path, choose prepend/append/system position, and trigger on every prompt, the first matching prompt in a shell session, or the first matching prompt after /compact or /clear
Proxy IPC socket retries once on broken connection before giving up

Tab restore now persists each terminal pane's repo root and last-known branch, and older saved tabs can fall back to repo grouping metadata so protected-folder repos keep their passive git badge without immediate live access
Selected-tab branch badge, tab git indicator, hover-card branch row, and repo path label now fall back to cached repo identity for protected-folder repos when live git probing is unavailable
Known repo identities now preserve last-known branch metadata across recent-repo reordering and settings import, preventing protected-folder branch labels from regressing to unknown

RepositoryModel now carries an AccessLevel (.live or .cached) so a model exists for every known repo including protected-directory repos. Eliminates the cachedIdentity fallback path and circular branch-recording dependency.

Terminal input-lag telemetry now excludes AI input-to-first-output round trips so responsiveness metrics and CPU-saturation heuristics stay focused on local PTY/UI delay while AI timing keeps its own logs
Runtime sessions now accumulate cumulative token usage and estimated USD cost as turns complete, including separate reasoning-output token accounting and configurable cost-threshold events
Repository pane turn summaries now show estimated turn cost, active duration, and average tokens per completed turn while a session is active

Registry-driven pre-commit/pre-push/local validation: staged secret and dependency policy, ruff autofix, JS/TS guardrails, legacy source/docs/design checks, affected-surface pre-push, conservative full-suite triggers, live dependency audits, registry-backed quality tests, content-hash caching, per-gate logs, and full local CI parity

A staged-features-csv quality gate runs scripts/check-features-csv.mjs to deterministically reject malformed features.csv rows (wrong column count, unquoted commas, invalid Status/Differentiator, blank lines) at commit time
Floating hint box showing keyboard shortcuts (⌘/) and report issue (⌥⌘I), positioned 4pt from tab bar and window edge
Adaptive clipboard polling, shared background drain, event-driven focus detection, timer leeway coalescing, wakeup stats logging
Active AI PTY log is flushed before tab_output reads it
PTY log tails are terminal-normalized before transcript parsing
SwiftPM package metadata excludes each directory's README.md from resource scanning so per-directory docs aren't bundled as app resources
Pre-commit review traces tab lifecycle steps, retries transient scripting calls, and confirms prompt submission before parsing output
Pre-commit review prefers stored repo AI events with full messages to detect the final structured review result before falling back to transcript scraping
Background snapshots can reuse cached remote transcript text, and notification logic treats elicitation plus tool/response failures as first-class interactive events
Split-tab AI resume restore binds each saved resume command to its owning terminal pane instead of routing through the rebuilt focused pane
Restore-time resume prefills validate pane directory and restored AI identity before insertion and skip mismatched stale targets
Pane-owned resume restore records scheduled, queued, delivered, rejected, and superseded outcomes per pane so stale retries stay diagnosable
Late stale retry callbacks no longer overwrite a newer pane's delivered restore outcome, so the ledger preserves the winning pane state
Cross-view Metal coordinator handoffs clear pending sync/present requests plus retry and deferred-sync state so a newly selected tab cannot briefly present stale work from the previous view
Scroll storms visible noninteractive windows and near-full-row redraw bursts force full Metal instance refreshes so stale incremental cell data cannot mix old glyphs into new shell text
Predicted local-echo cells are cleared on shell redraw-style output and when a terminal stops live presentation or interactivity, preventing duplicate typed characters from lingering on-screen
All user-facing AI events (permission, waiting_input, finished, failed, idle, tool_failed, response_failed, elicitation) trigger tab border/color highlights with 30s auto-clear
Native and AppleScript fallback notifications keep the title focused on tool and state while adding repo tab or directory context in the subtitle

FFI snapshot ships UTF-8 grapheme clusters with explicit width and continuation so ZWJ emoji, regional-indicator flags, VS16 emoji, and NFD combining sequences round-trip from Rust to the Metal/AppKit renderer intact; atlas re-keyed by cluster bytes and fragment shader wired for color-emoji RGBA

Rendered-output risk highlighting strips prompt and markdown decorations, requires token boundaries and executable-style prefixes, and avoids flagging prose mentions of dangerous commands as runnable shell lines

After each resize Chau7 re-delivers SIGWINCH to the PTY foreground process group a short coalesced moment later, so a full-screen TUI that booted during the resize and missed the kernel signal (e.g. Claude Code's Ink caching a stale width) re-reads the authoritative size instead of corrupting its diff-repainted output for the rest of the session

feature: iPhone Approval Polling and Push Setup
description: Remote iPhone app derives approval polling from relay base URLs, cleans up approval background tasks safely, requests standard notification permissions, and tracks explicit push entitlements plus iOS 18 deployment settings in the project
FFI resize binds a shared reference with atomic dimensions so a main-thread resize cannot alias concurrent drain-thread polls
Terminal creation duplicates env keys/values into C-owned buffers freed after the FFI call instead of escaping closure-scoped pointers
Agent dashboard tracker and cursor state is confined to a serial refresh queue so poll cycles and commit actions cannot race main-thread mutations
Prompt-injection session events post to the proxy asynchronously instead of blocking the main thread on a semaphore
Dictionary builds over persisted or transformable keys use first-wins uniquing so duplicate keys in stored data degrade gracefully instead of crashing
Saved tabs restore exactly once across all windows via first-occurrence-wins tab ID claims, preventing duplicated-window cascades
Quit reuses the cached autosave snapshot only when a structural fingerprint of live windows matches, so recent changes always persist
Restore prefers whichever of the file bundle and UserDefaults index carries the latest save token so stale bundles cannot resurrect old sessions
Restore bundle directory swap uses safe-save replaceItemAt and logs manifest or sidecar corruption instead of silently degrading
Terminal creation failure shows a localized error card with retry instead of a blank tab, and verifies the passwd shell exists before spawning
History store mutators are queue-confined and clearOlderThan decrements the cached count so trim can never over-delete valid records
iCloud settings sync applies only strictly-newer timestamped blobs, refuses newer format versions, and preserves local values for absent fields
Interactive bash gets --rcfile shell integration and all shells spawn in their intended working directory via FFI launch arguments
FFI selection coordinates clamp to grid bounds and PTY reader-pool spawn failure fails terminal creation cleanly instead of aborting

feature: Metal In-Flight Gate & GPU Error Recovery
description: Metal frames are gated one-in-flight so CPU buffer writes never race the GPU, and command-buffer errors force a full-refresh redraw
Glyph and ligature rasterization clip to their atlas slot so overhanging glyphs cannot corrupt neighboring cached glyphs
Backing-property changes reconfigure the glyph atlas scale and force a redraw so cross-display drags render crisply without a tab switch
Fully covered windows demote to the 1Hz background drain and re-expose forces an immediate grid sync
The reader pool drains all kernel-buffered PTY output after HUP before signalling closed so fast-exiting commands never lose tail output
PTY resize ioctls TIOCSWINSZ on a dup'd fd instead of taking the writer mutex so window drags never stall behind a backpressured write
Every AcrossWindows method dispatches to main before touching tab models, honoring the documented any-thread contract
Dev-server, file-tailer, shell-event, process-tree, and remote-IPC monitors confine their state to owning queues, closing five cross-thread races
File monitors re-arm after delete-replace with backoff, the tailer re-arms on rename with bounded reads, and the dylib loader retries after cooldown
Pinned per-tab snapshots, buffer duplicates, and search caches clear on demotion, close, and OS pressure, with a startup sweep of orphaned scrollback caches
GPU atlas and buffers of fully invisible windows go volatile under critical pressure with reclaim-safe rebuild on the next draw
The app polls its own footprint against a 4-12GB ceiling and synthesizes memory pressure before the OS signal arrives
chau7-proxy and chau7-remote exit when the parent app dies, and proxy auto-restart uses capped exponential backoff
The Rust acceleration crate (ANSI parse, pattern match, escape sanitize, command-risk detection) exports its symbols and is verified at build time
The dylib exports an ABI version and struct sizeof probes verified before symbol binding, plus an end-to-end integration test against the built dylib
Versions derive from git tags with loud failure, pinned Rust toolchain, inside-out signing without --deep, and a guard against new dead test gates
Remaining silent try? failures across settings, SSH, remote, telemetry, and injection paths now log via the Persist helpers
Log sink, event-drain generations, deallocation flag, poll diagnostics, usage snapshot, and reset-persist races are lock-guarded or generation-tokened

feature: Cache Caps & Dead Render API Removal
description: Clipboard and resolver caches are bounded, graphics abort buffers shrink, and the unused frame-pacing API is removed
The remaining dead #if !SWIFT_PACKAGE test files are ungated and repaired, bringing the package suite to 3,000+ tests with a guard against new gates
Resume prefill validation treats unresolved current AI identity as a match instead of rejecting, so corroboration lag does not lose the prefill
Resume prefill directory-ownership check canonicalizes both expected and live paths via symlink resolution, tilde expansion, and standardization before comparing
Warm-idle tabs flush scrollback to disk losslessly via ANSI capture and shrink to the viewport floor without changing render phase, then restore with colors on reselection
The restore pipeline re-resolves Claude/Codex session IDs from on-disk transcripts when autosave captured a synthetic identity, so tabs no longer come back without the resume prefill

feature: Live Process Tree Authoritative for AI-Running
description: isAIRunning treats a non-nil liveAgentName as authoritative so logos stay solid when an AI process is in the tab tree even after activeAppName is cleared on prompt-return

The Move to Idle Tabs action switches selection to a neighbor when invoked on the selected tab instead of silently no-op-ing, and the idle dropdown re-evaluates immediately via the observable suspendedTabIDs signal
The text side panel defaults to source view instead of runbook preview when opening a markdown file
The runbook view renders inline markdown bold, italic, code, and links via AttributedString instead of showing literal characters

The source/runbook toggle button is labeled Preview (Show Source when previewing) and an editor-pane-scoped Cmd+Shift+P shortcut flips between the two views, distinct from the global Cmd+Option+P command palette

The runbook styler recognizes - [ ] and - [x] task items, styling the box green when checked and dimmed when not, striking through completed task text, and toggling the box state when clicked in the source edit pane
Wrapped lines in markdown list items and blockquotes hang under their text content instead of the marker, via a per-paragraph head indent measured in the body font

Restore-time re-resolution scans both Claude and Codex transcripts when autosave captured no provider, so blank-state tabs are recovered from the on-disk session whose mtime is closest to the saved activity time or the newest available
CodexSessionResolver.sessionCandidates accepts a saved activity timestamp as the day-directory search anchor and a wider maxDays so the restore pipeline finds transcripts from week-old day folders
The restored AI tab logo stays legible against dark backgrounds when the AI process has exited, instead of fading to a near-invisible 0.35 opacity

aiDisplayAppName reads only lastAIProvider, and the process-tree subscription writes through to it via updateLastDetectedApp, so a single persisted field drives the AI tab logo across detection, live exit, and restore

Restore-time reResolveResumeCommand refuses to guess when the saved pane has nil provider, session, and command, and dedups its on-disk candidate against session ids already claimed by sibling tabs so two tabs sharing a directory never get the same claude --resume id

Resume prefill queued by restore falls back from the eager 0.3-3s backoff to a 5s heartbeat after retry 20 instead of silently abandoning pendingPrefillInput, so cold-boot tabs whose OSC 133 prompt takes longer than ~30s still get their resume command inserted once the shell is ready

selectTab walks the newly selected tab's terminal sessions and calls flushPendingPrefillInputIfReady on each, so a queued resume prefill is delivered the instant the user looks at the tab instead of waiting for the next OSC 133 prompt or view reattach

An authoritative interactive-attention observation reopens a session the reconciler had marked terminal, so long-lived agents like Codex that emit no raw lifecycle events still surface permission prompts after the first turn

One-call agent_launch MCP tool spawns N AI agents in fresh tabs, composing tab_create + tab_wait_ready + tab_exec + verified prompt injection with launch throttles, optional gh pr checkout, and per-agent prompt visibility/submission/running fields
Switch-to-tab shortcuts are user-selectable between Cmd+1-9, F1-F12 (up to 12 tabs), or both in Settings > Tabs, with the default staying Cmd+1-9

Visible-but-unfocused terminal views throttle grid-sync and draw rate to a configurable Background View Refresh Cap (default 42 fps) while staying event-driven, and the Active Tab Refresh Cap now actually caps the focused view
Tabs whose repo group points at a moved or renamed missing directory are re-tagged to their live git root on app activation, so a relocated repo no longer shows two identically named tab groups
The Metal ligature cache separates real-ligature hits from the unbounded miss keyspace so transient miss churn cannot evict and force re-rasterization of genuine ligatures
didChangeScreenParameters overlay-window re-clamping is debounced 250ms to coalesce the bursts macOS fires during a display reconfiguration

Single-byte printable ASCII cells resolve their Metal glyph through a flat style-indexed array instead of a per-cell Data allocation and dictionary hash, while multi-byte clusters keep the existing cache

Scoped single-use HMAC v2 tokens (per device/role/endpoint) in the Authorization header with constant-time verify and Durable-Object nonce replay defense; the Worker fails closed without a configured secret and the secret reaches iOS via the pairing payload
Relay rebuilt on Hibernatable WebSockets with per-device rate limiting, body/frame size caps, sanitized pending-state WebSocket backpressure, and reason-based APNs registration removal

features.csv is generated from docs/features.json (the single source of truth) by scripts/generate-features-csv.mjs; a staged-features-csv-generated gate runs --check to fail the commit when the committed CSV drifts from the manifest
